setting.note: Rhys writes the Caribbean back into Jane Eyre, giving the first wife her own island childhood. The anchor is Jamaica, where the creole heiress Antoinette grows up at the ruined Coulibri estate near Spanish Town after emancipation; the story then crosses to a honeymoon house on Dominica, Rhys's own birthplace, and ends far away in an English attic. It brings Jamaica, and the wider West Indies, into the atlas.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Jean Rhys, first published 1966. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: Wide Sargasso Sea, Jean Rhys (1966). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The blue-green of the sea that gives the book its name and separates every place in it from every other. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
